query_normalized: the impact of interoceptive awareness on emotional regulation during simulated stress psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on robotic engineering and sensor integration (proprioception/exteroception in soft robots), which is an off-domain application of "bodily awareness" distinct from the psychological construct of "interoceptive awareness" and "emotional regulation" in humans. This falls under the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords (e.g., "bodily awareness" in robotics vs. psychology) with no measurable connection to the user's mechanism, domain, or empirical set
